options.label: A
options.text: A force that opposes motion between two surfaces in contact

options.label: B
options.text: A force that always increases the speed of moving objects

options.label: C
options.text: A type of energy that makes objects move faster

options.label: D
options.text: A force that only acts on metal surfaces

explanation: Friction is a resistive force that acts between two surfaces in contact and opposes the direction of motion. It is present on many different types of surfaces, not just metal.
